rulings.Case Title: J. STEPHEN GRAZIANI VS. SUSAN CHAN DDS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 28, 2010, line 4. DEFENDANT SUSAN CHAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is denied. Failure to meet initial burden. No showing that subject statements are made about a public figure or in connection with an issue of public interest. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: J. STEPHEN GRAZIANI VS. SUSAN CHAN DDS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 28, 2010, line 4. DEFENDANT SUSAN CHAN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer overruled as to first cause of action. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the 2nd cause of action (Interference with Economic Advantage) and 3rd cause of action (Defamation). Plaintiff must plead both causes of action with greater specificity. Unclear whether plaintiff alleges interference with a current economic relationship, or prospective one, and with whom. Plaintiff fails to allege specifics of false statements, or provide any details. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to 4th cause of action (harassment) which is not a recognized cause of action, however plaintiff may amend to allege incidents, if any, that do constitute a recognized cause of action. If plaintiff wishes to pursue injunctive relief for civil harassment, he must follow the procedures set forth in CCP section 527.6. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: TERROIR HOTEL AND RESORT FUND, L.P.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Terroir Hotel And Resort Fund Lp'S Petition To Vacate Or Correct Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 28, 2010, line 8. Petitioner Terroir Hotel And Resort Fund LP'S Petition To Vacate Or Correct Arbitration Award is denied. Insufficient showing that the award was procured by "fraud or undue means" or that the "arbitrator exceeded his powers..." as set forth in CCP section 1286.2. None of the alleged errors are evident on the face of the award that would afford relief under CCP section 1286.6. The award is confirmed. =(302/CWW)
